name: New Mexican Breakfast Casserole
instructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C). Generously grease a small baking dish with butter or cooking spray.

2. Heat the Vegetable Oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the Frozen Hash Browns and diced Onion, spreading them in an even layer. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the hash browns are golden brown and crispy on the edges, about 10 minutes.

3. Meanwhile, cook the Bacon slices in a separate skillet over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, until evenly browned and crispy, about 10 minutes. Transfer to paper towels to drain excess grease, then crumble into bite-sized pieces.

4. Create layers in the prepared baking dish: start with the hash brown and onion mixture as the base, then add the crumbled Bacon pieces, followed by some shredded Cheddar Cheese, and top with the chopped Roasted Green Chile Peppers.

5. Carefully crack the Eggs directly on top of the layered ingredients, spacing them evenly. Sprinkle the remaining shredded Cheddar Cheese over everything.

6. Bake in the preheated oven until the egg whites are set, the yolks reach your desired doneness, and the cheese is melted and starting to turn golden brown, about 20 to 30 minutes.

7. Let the casserole rest for a few minutes, then serve hot with warm Tortillas on the side.
simpleInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven and grease a small baking dish.

2. Cook the Frozen Hash Browns and diced Onion in Vegetable Oil until golden and crispy.

3. Cook the Bacon until crispy, then drain and crumble.

4. Layer the hash browns, crumbled Bacon, some cheese, and chopped chiles in the dish.

5. Crack the Eggs on top and sprinkle with remaining cheese.

6. Bake until the Eggs are set and cheese is golden.

7. Serve hot with warm Tortillas.
